description: This schematic diagram illustrates the interaction between planar slip bands and grain boundaries (GB) in Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel during fatigue deformation. The image is divided by a prominent red vertical line representing a grain boundary, separating a "Soft-oriented grain" (left, beige background) from a "Hard-oriented grain" (right, light blue background). In the soft-oriented grain, multiple parallel dashed lines labeled "Planar slip bands" traverse the entire grain interior, marked with perpendicular "T" symbols indicating dislocation slip steps or stacking faults. These slip bands propagate toward the grain boundary and attempt to transmit into the hard-oriented grain. In the hard-oriented grain, the slip bands appear less dense and exhibit deflection or blockage at the grain boundary, suggesting orientation-dependent slip transfer mechanisms. The diagram visually represents how intragranular κ-carbide precipitation promotes planar slip character, leading to dislocation pile-ups at grain boundaries that act as barriers to slip transmission. This mechanism is critical for understanding the fatigue micromechanisms in aged Fe-Mn-Al-C steels, where the competition between slip band penetration and grain boundary strengthening determines the fatigue crack initiation resistance.
